hyperedges.label: Spec-Kit Workflow: Extensions, Binding Skills, and Slash Commands
Cross-Service Code-First Pattern: Polyrepo Rule, AMQP Subscribers, ClientAssignment Drift
DB Connect Skill Benchmarking: Snapshot, Benchmark, Evals
Entra External ID Auth Flow for Portal Cliente
Afianza Microservices AMQP Backbone
Graphify Incremental Update Pipeline
DEVPT-518 Core Data Model: client_team + client_team_assignment + change log
hyperedges.rationale: Three tables form the authoritative temporal data model for DEVPT-518; client_team_assignment_change is inserted within the same DB transaction as every mutation to client_team_assignment.

hyperedges.label: DEVPT-518 AMQP propagation: opened/closed events consumed by obligations-api and data-factory
hyperedges.rationale: Three AMQP entry/exit points of pgi-api for the client-team-assignment feature; opened and closed events propagate downstream to pd-service-obligations-api and pd-service-data-factory, while the onboarding subscriber creates assignments from external events.

hyperedges.label: DEVPT-518 authoritative document trinity: spec + er-diagram + data-model
hyperedges.rationale: er-diagram.md is the authoritative data model; spec.md v2 is the authoritative functional spec; decisions.md (ADRs) records all architectural decisions. These three documents are the source of truth for the feature and all other artifacts must align to them.

hyperedges.label: Temporal assignment model: half-open intervals + rewrite frontier + governing model
AMQP cross-service flow: pgi-api publishes → data-factory persists → jira-adapter syncs / obligations reassigns
US-01 implementation: BE + FE + setup must converge

nodes.norm_label: watch mode with debounce (auto-rebuild on code changes, 3s default)

nodes.label: Node ID Format Convention
nodes.rationale: IDs are deterministic from label+path to avoid ghost duplicates across chunks. Uses parent_dir+filename_stem to match AST extractor output. Chunk numbers are never appended.

nodes.label: Confidence Score Rubric
nodes.rationale: Discrete values (0.95/0.85/0.75/0.65/0.55) prevent the bimodal collapse to 0.5/0.85 seen in production. EXTRACTED=1.0 always; AMBIGUOUS=0.1-0.3.

nodes.label: Graphify Post-Commit Hook
nodes.rationale: Auto-rebuilds graph after every commit without background process. Detects changed code files via git diff and re-runs AST extraction only on those. Doc/image changes are not picked up by hook.

nodes.label: Graphify Query Expansion via Vocab
nodes.rationale: Query CLI uses literal substring+IDF match with no stemming. Expanding against actual graph vocabulary before traversal prevents 0-hit queries when user language differs from graph labels.

nodes.label: Graphify Incremental Update
nodes.rationale: Only re-extracts changed files to save tokens. Code-only changes skip LLM semantic extraction entirely. Prunes old nodes for changed files before inserting fresh AST to prevent ghost-node duplicates (#1178).

nodes.label: extension_companyIds Custom Claim
nodes.rationale: Entra External ID native attributes are scalar; must store as CSV string or Graph schema extension array. Used for per-client-company data isolation across all NestJS services serving External ID tokens.

nodes.label: ExternalIDStrategy (af-nest-module-auth)
nodes.rationale: Extends af-nest-module-auth with ExternalID passport strategy. Uses passport-jwt+jwks-rsa (not jose, which is ESM-only). Conditional registration in forAsyncRoot prevents crash when tenantId not provided. useClaimsOnly flag prevents silent fallback that would open privilege escalation surface.

nodes.label: CompanyIsolationService
nodes.rationale: Request-scoped service validating X-Active-Company header against companyIds array from token. Strict includes() comparison prevents CSV injection and prefix-match attacks. Incompatible with AMQP consumers (no HTTP context).

nodes.label: MultiIssuerGuard
nodes.rationale: Routes to correct guard by peeking iss claim without verifying signature. Uses startsWith not includes to prevent evil.com/ciamlogin.com.phish bypass. Has 8KB cap, 3-part JWS check, alg allow-list before decode.

nodes.label: AzureADStrategy Issuer v2 Fix
nodes.rationale: Migrates from hardcoded STS v1 issuer (sts.windows.net) to v2 (login.microsoftonline.com). acceptLegacyV1Tokens: true during transition absorbs cached MSAL tokens. Pre-flight F8 blocks deploy until accessTokenAcceptedVersion: 2 is confirmed.

nodes.label: OperationTaxReturns
nodes.rationale: Uses EntityManager directly (not TaxReturnService) because assertBillingInvariants is too strict for historical data. Employee lookup uses in-memory cache (Map<fullNameLower, Employee>) to avoid N queries for ~600 rows.

nodes.label: CompanyIsolationGuard (APP_GUARD global)
nodes.rationale: Default-deny APP_GUARD registered after ExternalIDGuard. Endpoints opt out with @SkipIsolation(). Not request-scoped (instantiated once, operates on ExecutionContext). companyIds:[] triggers awaiting-access 403 without audit log; invalid header triggers company-isolation 403 WITH audit log.

nodes.label: MSAL Frontend Auth (mp-app-portalcliente-web)
nodes.rationale: main.tsx async init (pca.initialize() -> handleRedirectPromise() -> render) is mandatory; rendering MsalProvider before initialize() silently breaks redirect flows. Single PCA instance (one External ID tenant). MSAL v4 aligned with backoffice for copy-paste of patterns.

nodes.label: R-01: Composite endpoint for atomic replacement
nodes.rationale: Atomic composite endpoint POST /clients/{id}/teams/{teamId}/operations chosen over two separate calls (DELETE + POST) to avoid intermediate inconsistent state and reduce latency.
nodes.norm_label: r-01: composite endpoint for atomic replacement

nodes.label: R-02: inClientSince calculated on-demand server-side
nodes.rationale: On-demand single aggregate query per request chosen over materialized view or Redis cache due to small volumes (~50k active rows max).

nodes.label: R-03: Two-step migration to drop start_date/end_date from client_team
nodes.rationale: Two-deploy strategy: Deploy A adds new columns without touching old ones; Deploy B drops after consumer audit. Avoids breakage from unknown consumers.
nodes.norm_label: r-03: two-step migration to drop start_date/end_date from client_team

nodes.label: R-04: AMQP publish after flush without outbox pattern
nodes.rationale: Publish after flush accepted for small dual-write risk window (<10ms); reconciliation via daily data-factory snapshot mitigates lost events.
nodes.norm_label: r-04: amqp publish after flush without outbox pattern

nodes.label: R-05: One E2E test file per User Story
nodes.rationale: 1:1 spec-to-test traceability and fault isolation.
nodes.norm_label: r-05: one e2e test file per user story

nodes.label: Temporal Assignment Model (tramos con dateFrom/dateTo)
nodes.rationale: Replaces destructive edit of team rows with temporal intervals. Every change materializes as close+open of spans.

nodes.label: Governing model: department-wide snapshot sequence
nodes.rationale: Each snapshot governs interval [D, N) where N is the next snapshot dateFrom across the entire (client, department). Preserves future snapshots byte-for-byte.

hyperedges.label: Spec-Kit Workflow: Extensions, Binding Skills, and Slash Commands
Cross-Service Code-First Pattern: Polyrepo Rule, AMQP Subscribers, ClientAssignment Drift
DB Connect Skill Benchmarking: Snapshot, Benchmark, Evals
Entra External ID Auth Flow for Portal Cliente
Afianza Microservices AMQP Backbone
Graphify Incremental Update Pipeline
DEVPT-518 Core Data Model: client_team + client_team_assignment + change log
hyperedges.rationale: Three tables form the authoritative temporal data model for DEVPT-518; client_team_assignment_change is inserted within the same DB transaction as every mutation to client_team_assignment.

hyperedges.label: DEVPT-518 AMQP propagation: opened/closed events consumed by obligations-api and data-factory
hyperedges.rationale: Three AMQP entry/exit points of pgi-api for the client-team-assignment feature; opened and closed events propagate downstream to pd-service-obligations-api and pd-service-data-factory, while the onboarding subscriber creates assignments from external events.

hyperedges.label: DEVPT-518 authoritative document trinity: spec + er-diagram + data-model
hyperedges.rationale: er-diagram.md is the authoritative data model; spec.md v2 is the authoritative functional spec; decisions.md (ADRs) records all architectural decisions. These three documents are the source of truth for the feature and all other artifacts must align to them.

hyperedges.label: Temporal assignment model: half-open intervals + rewrite frontier + governing model
AMQP cross-service flow: pgi-api publishes → data-factory persists → jira-adapter syncs / obligations reassigns
US-01 implementation: BE + FE + setup must converge
